page: Microsoft Azure FX96ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64 and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 1832.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 19.08 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data highlights the exceptional performance of the FX96ms_v2 across multiple dimensions. In memory operations, it achieves top-tier status with cached small-block read bandwidth of 7653.09 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth of 448.55 GB/sec. CPU performance is similarly robust, with a Passmark CPU score of 103969.11 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 22291.0, both placing in the top 10% tier. The processor's support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) drives strong results in specialized workloads, including a top-tier score of 138937.13 in the Passmark CPU extended instructions test and high throughput in LLM inference prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX96ms_v2 represents a premium tier of hardware designed for resource-dense configurations where high-frequency compute and massive memory capacity must be tightly coupled. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachment, which may introduce latency and cost tradeoffs depending on the storage architecture selected. This server is highly optimized for memory-intensive and compute-heavy workloads, such as large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure FX96ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance is equipped with 1832.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 19.08 GB. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is premium storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance, particularly in memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-core CPU execution, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. The FX96ms_v2 is engineered for demanding, memory-heavy enterprise workloads, making it highly suitable for large-scale database management, in-memory data structures, and complex analytical processing.
